evals.name: eval-install-check
evals.prompt: I want to compile a Verilog-A file with OpenVAF but I'm not sure if I have it installed. Help me check and install it if needed. I'm on Windows.
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step instructions: check PATH, download from openvaf.semimod.de, rename to .exe, install MSVC Build Tools, verify with openvaf --help.

evals.name: eval-simple-resistor
evals.prompt: Write a simple nonlinear resistor model in Verilog-A (resistance depends on temperature: R = R0*(1 + tc1*(T-Tnom))). Then compile it with OpenVAF and create an ngspice netlist to test it with a DC sweep.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with temperature-dependent resistor, openvaf compile command, ngspice netlist with pre_osdi, N-prefixed instance, .model card, DC sweep.

evals.name: eval-diode-model
evals.prompt: Create a simple exponential diode model in Verilog-A with parameters Is and N. Compile with OpenVAF and simulate forward I-V curve in ngspice from 0 to 0.8V.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with I=Is*(exp(V/(N*Vt))-1), limexp for convergence, openvaf command, ngspice netlist with proper OSDI loading, DC sweep plot.

evals.name: eval-mosfet-iv
evals.prompt: I have a square-law MOSFET .va file. Show me how to compile it and generate Id-Vds family curves in ngspice with Vgs stepping from 0.5V to 2V.
evals.expected_output: openvaf compile command, ngspice netlist with nested DC sweep (Vds outer, Vgs inner), pre_osdi loading, N-prefix instance, plot command.

evals.name: eval-debug-no-N-prefix
evals.prompt:
My OSDI model loads fine but the device seems to be ignored - no current flows. Here's my netlist:

.control
pre_osdi ./myresistor.osdi
dc V1 0 5 0.1
plot -i(V1)
.endc

V1 in 0 DC 1
.model r1k myresistor r=1000
R1 in 0 r1k

.end

What's wrong?
evals.expected_output: Identify that R1 should be Nr1 — OSDI instances must start with N. Without it, ngspice treats R1 as a built-in resistor and ignores the OSDI model.

evals.name: eval-behavioral-reject
evals.prompt: I wrote a VCO in Verilog-A that uses @(cross()) and idtmod(). Can I compile it with OpenVAF and simulate in ngspice?
evals.expected_output: Explain that OpenVAF does NOT support @(cross()) — it only handles the compact-model subset. VCOs are behavioral models and need a full Verilog-AMS simulator. Suggest alternatives.

evals.name: eval-varactor-charge
evals.prompt: Write a MOS varactor model with C(V) = Cmin + (Cmax-Cmin)/2 * (1+tanh((V-V0)/Vs)). Use charge-based formulation. Compile with OpenVAF and set up an AC simulation in ngspice.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with ddt(Q) contribution, series R for Q-factor, openvaf command, ngspice AC sweep netlist.

evals.name: eval-full-workflow
evals.prompt: Walk me through the complete workflow from scratch: I want to model a voltage-controlled current source (VCCS) in Verilog-A with gain parameter gm, compile it, and verify it works in ngspice with a transient simulation.
evals.expected_output: Complete .va source, openvaf compile, ngspice netlist with sin source driving control voltage, pre_osdi, N-prefix instance, transient analysis, plot of output current.
